data.title: Walmart Continues Market Share Gains Despite Slower Q4 Growth, UBS Says
data.description:
Walmart continues to gain market share and expand its alternative revenue streams despite a slight slowdown in sales growth during Q4, UBS Securities said in a report emailed Friday.

Walmart reported fiscal Q4 adjusted earnings Thursday of $0.66 per diluted share, up from $0.60 a year ago, with revenue rising to $180.55 billion from $173.39 billion.

The retail giant reported a 4.6% increase in US comparable sales, though eCommerce growth moderated to 20% from 22% in recent quarters. Walmart still exceeded the mid-point of its EPS guidance by about 11%, reinforcing investor confidence in its long-term strategy, UBS said.

The company's transformation efforts, including growth in advertising, marketplace sales, and store-fulfilled delivery, are driving profitability, with alternative revenue streams adding over $250 million to operating income and global advertising revenue rising 29% in Q4, UBS said.

Walmart projected 2025 EPS between $2.50 and $2.60, below the consensus estimate of $2.77, citing a stable consumer outlook but ongoing product mix pressures, the report said. "While [Walmart's] guidance was below consensus, we think it is a prudent step for the company to take a conservative stance, considering the uncertainties in the macro," it added.

Bulls see the pullback in Walmart's stock as a buying opportunity, expecting continued earnings growth. Bears, however, argue that valuation remains high and macroeconomic uncertainties could pressure margins, UBS said.

Looking ahead, Walmart expects steady sales growth of 3-4% in 2025, with continued expansion in eCommerce and alternative businesses, UBS said.

UBS maintained a buy rating on Walmart and adjusted its price target to $112 from $113.

data.title: Stocks Extend Declines on Weak US Economic News
data.description:
The S&P 500 Index today is down -0.67%, the Dow Jones Industrials Index is down -0.91%, and the Nasdaq 100 Index is down -0.72%. March E-mini S&P futures (ESH25) are down -0.60%, and March E-mini Nasdaq futures (NQH25) are down -0.62%. 

Stocks today are under pressure, with the S&P 500 sliding to a 1-week low and the Dow Jones Industrials falling to a 1-month low. Some negative corporate news is weighing on stocks, with Akamai Technologies is down more than -14% after forecasting full-year revenue below consensus. Also, UnitedHealth Group is down more than -8% after the Wall Street Journal reported that the US Department of Justice has launched an investigation into the company’s Medicare billing practices.

Stocks extended their losses today after US consumer sentiment in Feb unexpectedly fell to a 15-month low. Also, today’s US economic news signaled slower activity combined with higher inflation expectations, negative factors for stocks.

The US Feb S&P Global manufacturing PMI rose +0.4 to an 8-month high of 51.6, stronger than expectations of 51.4. However, the Feb S&P Global services PMI unexpectedly fell -3.2 to 49.7, weaker than expectations of an increase to 53.0 and the steepest pace of contraction in 2 years.

The University of Michigan US Feb consumer sentiment index was revised lower by -3.1 to a 15-month low of 64.7, weaker than expectations of no change at 67.8.

The University of Michigan US Feb 1-year inflation expectations remained unchanged at 4.3%, right on expectations. However, the Feb 5-10 year inflation expectations unexpectedly accelerated to a 29-year high of 3.5%, higher than expectations of no change at 3.3%.

US Jan existing home sales fell -4.9% m/m to 4.08 million, weaker than expectations of -2.6% to 4.13 million.

The markets are discounting the chances at 2% for a -25 bp rate cut at the next FOMC meeting on March 18-19.

Overseas stock markets today are higher. The Euro Stoxx 50 is up +0.02%. China’s Shanghai Composite Index climbed to a 7-week high and closed up +0.85%. Japan’s Nikkei Stock 225 recovered from a 2-1/2 week low and closed up +0.26%.

Interest Rates

March 10-year T-notes (ZNH25) today are up by +8 ticks. The 10-year T-note yield is down by -5.1 bp to 4.455%. March T-note prices are climbing today on carryover support from gains in 10-year German bunds. Also, today’s -1% decline in WTI crude oil to a three-session low is pressuring inflation expectations, a supportive factor for T-notes. T-note prices raced to their high after today’s US economic reports showed Feb consumer sentiment dropped to a 15-month low and Jan existing home sales fell more than expected. 

European government bond yields today are moving lower. The 10-year German bund yield is down -6.4 bp to 2.470%. The 10-year UK gilt yield is down -1.9 bp to 4.588%.

The Eurozone Feb S&P manufacturing PMI rose +0.7 to 46.6, stronger than expectations of 47.0. The Feb S&P composite PMI was unchanged at 50.2, weaker than expectations of an increase to 50.5.

Swaps are discounting the chances at 98% for a -25 bp rate cut by the ECB at the March 6 policy meeting.

US Stock Movers

Akamai Technologies is down more than -14% to lead losers in the S&P 500 after forecasting full-year revenue of $4.00 billion-$4.20 billion, below the consensus of $4.25 billion. 

UnitedHealth Group is down more than -8% to lead losers in the Dow Jones Industrials after the Wall Street Journal reported that the US Department of Justice has launched an investigation into the company’s Medicare billing practices.

Block is down more than -15% after forecasting Q1 gross profit of $2.32 billion, weaker than the consensus of $2.38 billion. 

NU Holdings Ltd is down more than -14% after reporting Q4 net income of $552.6 million, below the consensus of $566.4 million. 

Globant SA is down more than -24% after forecasting Q1 revenue of $618 million-$628 million, below the consensus of $637.8 million. 

RingCentral is down more than -7% after forecasting Q1 adjusted EPS of 93 cents to 97 cents, weaker than the consensus of $1.00. 

Rivian Automotive is down more than -6% after saying it expects about 8,000 vehicle deliveries in Q1, below the more than 14,000 it made in Q4.

Crowdstrike Holdings is down more than -3% after Bloomberg News reports the Justice Department and the SEC are investigating a $32 million deal between Crowdstrike and a technology distributor to provide cybersecurity to the Internal Revenue Service.

MercadoLibre is up more than +6% to lead gainers in the Nasdaq 100 after reporting Q4 net revenue of $6.10 billion, better than the consensus of $5.96 billion. 

PDD Holdings is up more than +5% after better-than-expected earnings from Alibaba lifted US-listed Chinese stocks. 

Booking Holdings is up more than +4% to lead gainers in the S&P 500 after reporting Q4 revenue of $5.47 billion, stronger than the consensus of $5.18 billion.

Innodata is up more than +26% after reporting Q4 diluted EPS of 31 cents, well above the consensus of 22 cents. 

Five9 Inc is up more than +3% after reporting Q4 adjusted EPS of 79 cents, better than the consensus of 70 cents, and forecast full-year adjusted EPS of $2.58-$2.62, stronger than the consensus of $2.55. 

Consolidated Edison  is up more than +2% after reporting Q4 adjusted EPS of 98 cents, stronger than the consensus of 95 cents. 

Unity Software is up more than +2% after HSBC upgraded the stock to buy from hold with a price target of $34. 

Live Nation Entertainment is up more than +1% after reporting Q4 revenue of $5.68 billion, above the consensus of $5.63 billion. 

Earnings Reports (2/21/2025)

AdvanSix Inc (ASIX), AG Mortgage Investment Trust Inc (MITT), Alamo Group Inc (ALG), Altair Engineering Inc (ALTR), Amedisys Inc (AMED), Arbor Realty Trust Inc (ABR), Atmus Filtration Technologies (ATMU), Balchem Corp (BCPC), Boise Cascade Co (BCC), C4 Therapeutics Inc (CCCC), Cross Country Healthcare Inc (CCRN), Edgewise Therapeutics Inc (EWTX), Federal Agricultural Mortgage (AGM), Gencor Industries Inc (GENC), H&E Equipment Services Inc (HEES), Hawaiian Electric Industries Inc (HE), MGE Energy Inc (MGEE), ModivCare Inc (MODV), Oil States International Inc (OIS), Spirit AeroSystems Holdings In (SPR), St Joe Co/The (JOE), Standard Motor Products Inc (SMP), Sunstone Hotel Investors Inc (SHO), Telephone and Data Systems Inc (TDS), TXNM Energy Inc (TXNM), Uniti Group Inc (UNIT).

On the date of publication, Rich Asplund did not have (either directly or indirectly) positions in any of the securities mentioned in this article. All information and data in this article is solely for informational purposes. For more information please view the Barchart Disclosure Policyhere.

data.title: AlphaValue/Baader Europe Kicks Off Evolution AB Coverage at Buy on Market Dominance, Attractive Valuation
data.description:
AlphaValue/Baader Europe commenced its coverage of Evolution AB with a buy rating and a price target of 1,418 kronor, highlighting the Swedish online gambling company's market dominance.

"Despite near-term execution risks, we believe regulatory expansion, Evolution's superior unit economics, product innovation, oligopolistic market positioning, and resilient cash flow generation make it an attractive investment proposition," analysts said Friday.

Evolution currently holds a 60% to 90% share in the live casino market, its core growth driver, through which it generates 85% of revenue. The company's expansion and newly regulated markets such as Brazil and the US are also seen as positive drivers for potential market outperformance.

"Evolution trades at very cheap valuations given the strong fundamentals," analysts added. "At 13.2x P/E (60% discount to historical averages), the stock offers a compelling risk-reward opportunity."

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 11 AM ET
data.description:
Home Sales Fell 4.9% in January, Extending Slump in Housing Market

Home prices and mortgage rates, which were a drag on home sales for most of 2024, stayed elevated and put off many buyers.

U.S. Consumer Confidence Falls Back on Fears of Tariff-Induced Price Increases

The University of Michigan's index of consumer sentiment tumbled to 64.7 at the end of February, well below January's 71.7. It also was weaker than economists' expectations of 67.8.

Dow Moves Lower as UnitedHealth Stock Drops

The Dow fell moderately on probe of UnitedHealth by the Justice Department, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100 posted muted moves.

Europe's Economic Stagnation Persists, Latest Business Surveys Say

The eurozone economy continued to flatline in February, torn between signs of revival in Germany and sharp decline in France.

Chinese Stocks Rally on Strong Alibaba Earnings, DeepSeek Optimism

Upbeat earnings by big tech companies have added to a recent rally driven by Xi Jinping's meeting with top corporate executives and the AI hype around DeepSeek.

In the Trump Era, Flying Green Doesn't Matter Anymore

The president's endorsement has pushed up air-taxi stocks, but development of all types of zero-emission aircraft is falling by the wayside.

U.K. Retail Sales Rise for First Time in Five Months

Retailers in the U.K. reported a brighter start to the new year, with sales rising after four straight months of falls, largely from a recovery in the food segment.

French Industry Sentiment Improves Despite Tariff Threat

The mood in France's factories brightened a little this month as borrowing costs come down, and despite the threat of tariffs on exports to the U.S.

Why Is Warren Buffett Hoarding So Much Cash?

Investors are poised to study the Berkshire Hathaway chairman's annual letter for insights about the stock market.

The Latest Dilemma Facing Finance Chiefs: What to Tell Investors About Tariffs

CFOs are weighing how much information to share about the impact of tariffs, especially as many are yet to take effect and the landscape continues to evolve.

data.title: Coinbase Stock Falls. The SEC Is Giving Up Its Case Against the Crypto Exchange. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Adam Clark

Coinbase shares were rising early Friday after the cryptocurrency exchange said the Securities and Exchange Commission was ready to drop its lawsuit against the company.

Coinbase said Friday that enforcers at the SEC will recommend dismissal of a two-year-old lawsuit which sought to regulate the company as a stock exchange.

"SEC staff have agreed to dismiss their case against us (pending Commission approval)," the company said in a post on social-media site X.

Coinbase shares were rising 4.3% in premarket trading.

Dismissing the Coinbase lawsuit still requires a commission vote. The company expects that to happen next week, according to Coinbase's chief legal officer Paul Grewal, The Wall Street Journal reported.

Write to Adam Clark at adam.clark@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Update: Arbor Realty Trust Shares Fall After Lower Q4 Distributable Earnings, Net Interest Income
data.description:
(Updates with the latest stock price movement in the headline and the first paragraph.)

Arbor Realty Trust's shares fell more than 11% in recent Friday trading after the company reported lower Q4 distributable earnings and net interest income compared to the prior year.

The company reported Q4 distributable earnings Friday of $0.40 per diluted share, down from $0.51 a year earlier.

Four analysts polled by FactSet expected $0.38.

Net interest income for the quarter ended Dec. 31 was $82.9 million, down from $103.6 million a year earlier.

Comparable analyst estimates were not readily available.

The company said it will distribute an unchanged quarterly dividend of $0.43 per share, payable March 21 to shareholders of record on March 7.

data.title: Akamai Shares Fall on Slowing 2025 Growth
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Akamai Technologies shares slipped after the company said it expects revenue growth this year to be lower than Wall Street had anticipated.

The stock fell 15% to $83.81 Friday morning, hitting a 52-week low earlier in the session and on pace for its largest one-day percentage decline since 2017.

The cloud-computing and cybersecurity provider said late Thursday that it expects 2025 revenue at $4 billion to $4.2 billion, or a 2.7% increase at the midpoint--below the 6.8% climb to $4.26 billion that analysts polled by FactSet had predicted.

Akamai also said it expects 2025 adjusted earnings per share of $6 to $6.40, behind the $6.82 Wall Street had been forecasting.

The company expects headwinds from TikTok, which it said was its largest customer for content-delivery network services. The social-media platform is planning to move its content delivery internally, likely shaving off 1 to 2 percentage points of Akamai's revenue growth in 2025, D.A. Davidson analyst Rudy Kessinger said.

Akamai also plans to move about $50 million of its cloud-computing business to partners, which is expected to reduce revenue growth in that business to around 15%, down from 25% in 2024. The company said it wants to focus more on its core business.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Weave Communications Price Target Raised to $20.00/Share From $17.00 by Raymond James
Base Metal Prices Fall But Remain Up on Week on Tariff Concerns — Market Talk
data.description: Base metal prices fall, with LME three-month copper down 0.2% at $9,539 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum down 1.6% at $2,686 a ton. Prices remain elevated however, with copper up 0.85% and aluminum up 1.9% on week. President Trump's tariff policies prompted a squeeze in New York traded copper prices, which saw futures prices spike to a nine-month high last week. This widened the U.S. premium over LME prices to more than $1,000 a ton, Saxo Bank's Ole Hansen says in a note. Since then, prices have started to drift lower with the premium narrowing, though it remains significantly elevated, Hansen writes. Recent copper market actions have been mostly about U.S. commodities exchange Comex buying to preempt tariffs, more than actual end-user demand, Hansen adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

data.title: SIMON PROPERTY GROUP INC /DE/ SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Simon Property Group Inc. (NYSE: SPG), a leading global real estate company specializing in retail properties, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current market environment.

Financial Highlights

Simon Property Group Inc. reported robust financial performance for the year ended December 31, 2024. Key financial metrics include:

  • Operating Income: $3,092.8 million, reflecting strong operational performance and improved core fundamentals.
  • Consolidated Net Income: $2,729.0 million, driven by improved operating performance and solid core business fundamentals.
  • Diluted EPS: $7.26, an increase from $6.98 in 2023, primarily due to improved operating performance and increased lease income.
  • FFO of the Operating Partnership: $4,876.8 million, indicating robust financial health and operational efficiency.

Business Highlights

Simon Property Group Inc. demonstrated strong business performance across its portfolio. Key operational highlights include:

  • Revenue Segments: The company generates the majority of its lease income from retail, dining, entertainment, and other tenants, including fixed minimum lease consideration, fixed common area maintenance reimbursements, and variable lease consideration based on tenants’ reported sales.
  • Geographical Performance: As of December 31, 2024, Simon Property Group owned or held an interest in 194 income-producing properties in the United States, including malls, Premium Outlets, and The Mills. Internationally, the company had ownership in 35 Premium Outlets and Designer Outlet properties primarily located in Asia, Europe, and Canada.
  • Sales Units: During the twelve months ended December 31, 2024, Simon signed 1,149 new leases and 2,549 renewal leases across its U.S. Malls and Premium Outlets portfolio, covering approximately 13.5 million square feet.
  • New Product Launches: On August 15, 2024, Simon opened Tulsa Premium Outlets, a 338,472 square foot center in Tulsa, Oklahoma.
  • Future Outlook: Simon Property Group plans to continue focusing on high-quality real estate across the retail spectrum, selectively developing new properties in underserved markets, and partnering with established real estate companies for international investments.

Strategic Initiatives

Simon Property Group Inc. has undertaken several strategic initiatives to enhance its portfolio and manage its capital structure effectively. Key initiatives include:

  • Strategic Acquisitions and Dispositions: In 2024, the company acquired the remaining interest in Smith Haven Mall and an additional interest in Miami International Mall, resulting in the consolidation of these properties. Additionally, Simon completed the acquisition of two luxury outlet destinations in Italy in early 2025. The company also disposed of interests in two consolidated properties and one unconsolidated entity in 2024, aligning with its strategy to divest non-core assets.
  • Capital Management: Simon Property Group actively managed its capital structure through various debt and equity activities. The company completed several debt issuances and redemptions, including the issuance of $1.0 billion senior unsecured notes with a fixed interest rate of 4.75% and the redemption of $900 million 3.38% senior unsecured notes. The company maintained a robust liquidity position with an aggregate available borrowing capacity of approximately $8.2 billion under its Credit Facilities. Simon also paid dividends totaling $8.10 per share for 2024 and authorized a new $2.0 billion common stock repurchase program in 2024, replacing the previous program.
  • Future Outlook: Looking ahead, Simon Property Group plans to continue its focus on strategic acquisitions and redevelopment projects to drive growth. The company anticipates generating positive cash flow from operations in 2025 and expects to fund capital projects with cash flows from operations. Simon aims to maintain its investment-grade credit ratings and has outlined a capital strategy that includes generating necessary capital for growth, maintaining flexibility to access various forms of capital, and managing its financial structure to support its strategic objectives.

Challenges and Risks

Simon Property Group Inc. faces several significant risks that could materially and adversely affect its business operations and financial condition. Key risks include:

  • Market Risks: The company is exposed to conditions that adversely affect the general retail environment, such as government policies, tariffs, energy prices, and global events like international trade disputes and conflicts in Ukraine and Israel. These factors could impact consumer spending and demand for retail space.
  • Operational Risks: The loss of anchor stores or large nationally recognized tenants could materially impact the company's properties by reducing customer traffic and sales. Tenant bankruptcies pose a risk of lease terminations and financial losses. Additionally, vacant space and the inability to lease newly developed properties at desired rents could adversely affect the company's financial performance.
  • Regulatory Risks: The company must comply with REIT requirements, which could limit acquisition opportunities and require liquidation of investments. Changes in tax laws or failure to maintain REIT status could result in adverse tax consequences.
  • Emerging Risks: The rise of e-commerce and changing consumer preferences pose a competitive threat to traditional retail properties. Epidemics, pandemics, and public health crises could significantly impact the company's operations and financial condition. Climate change presents risks related to natural disasters and increased insurance costs.
  • Financial Risks: The company has a substantial debt burden, and disruptions in capital markets could increase the cost of capital and affect access to financing. Rising interest rates could increase interest costs on variable rate debt and impact refinancing capabilities.
  • Technological Risks: The use of artificial generative intelligence technologies presents risks related to the control and confidentiality of proprietary business information. Cybersecurity threats, including data breaches and cyber-attacks, could disrupt operations and compromise sensitive information.

Management has identified several strategies to address these challenges and risks:

  • Tenant Retention and Leasing: The company focuses on attracting and retaining high-quality tenants and expanding existing locations to enhance profitability.
  • Capital Strategy: A three-fold capital strategy is employed to generate necessary capital, maintain flexibility in accessing various forms of capital, and preserve investment-grade credit ratings.
  • Operational Improvements: Efforts are made to reduce operating expenses through economies of scale and enhance consumer traffic through marketing initiatives.
  • Risk Management: The company uses interest rate hedging arrangements to manage exposure to interest rate volatility and maintains insurance coverage to mitigate risks associated with natural disasters and other potential losses.

SEC Filing: SIMON PROPERTY GROUP INC /DE/ [ SPG ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: Wolfe Research Adjusts CBRE Group Price Target to $161 From $169

data.title: Legend Power Systems Fiscal Q1 Loss Narrows on Higher Revenues
data.description:
Legend Power Systems ,on Friday said its fiscal first-quarter loss narrowed as revenue surged.

The company, which provides an energy management platform, said its loss in the quarter ended Dec.31 fell 4% to $1.03 million, from $1.08 million, in the prior year period. Legend Power said a higher gross margin, and cost-cutting measures resulted in a narrower loss.

Revenue surged to $82,000, from $1,500 in the year-prior quarter. The higher revenue was primarily due to due to the fulfillment of SmartGATE units, the company said.

"The first quarter of 2025 marks a pivotal moment for Legend Power Systems as we build on the momentum generated over the past year," said Chief Executive Randy Buchamer. "Building owners require their facilities to operate optimally at all times, and SmartGATE has proven its ability to enhance reliability and efficiency."

The company's shares were last seen down $0.01 to $0.19 on the TSX Venture Exchange.

data.title: Keurig Dr Pepper Poised to Post 'Quiet' Q4 Results, RBC Says
data.description:
Keurig Dr Pepper is expected to post a "quiet" quarter and show steady growth in US refreshment beverages during Q4 while facing ongoing challenges in its coffee segment, RBC Capital Markets said in a note Friday.

The company may fall short of its ambitious topline growth target this year, a risk already factored into current estimates, the investment firm said.

For 2025, management is likely to maintain guidance similar to last quarter, with gains from refreshment drinks and the GHOST acquisition, despite weak coffee sales, RBC said.

Coffee sentiment remains negative because of rising commodity costs, but KDP's valuation is already higher than usual compared to peers, RBC analysts noted.

Despite current coffee challenges, RBC analysts highlighted the company's strong carbonated soft drink portfolio, growing international business, robust innovation pipeline, and an existing base of coffee brewers that could support future growth.

External challenges include potential FX headwinds, political uncertainties affecting trade, and inflation pressures, especially in the coffee sector, according to the note.

RBC maintained an outperform rating for Keurig Dr Pepper with a $42 price target.

data.title: European Equities Traded in the US as American Depositary Receipts Edge Higher in Friday Trading
data.description:
European equities traded in the US as American depositary receipts were slightly higher late Friday morning, edging 0.11% higher to 1,405.16 on the S&P Europe Select ADR Index, which is flat for the week.

From continental Europe, the gainers were led by pharmaceutical company Novo Nordisk and accommodations booking site trivago , which rose 5.3% and 2% respectively. They were followed by brewing company Anheuser-Busch InBev and telecommunications operator Telefonica , which were up 1.4% and 1% respectively.

The decliners from continental Europe were led by medical device maker EDAP TMS and internet advertising firm Criteo , which fell 4.4% and 3.2% respectively. They were followed by oil and gas companies TotalEnergies and Eni , which were down 1.7% and 1.4% respectively.

From the UK and Ireland, the gainers were led by biopharmaceutical company Verona Pharma and alcoholic beverage company Diageo , which increased 5% and 2.3% respectively. They were followed by biopharmaceutical company NuCana and financial services company Lloyds Banking Group , which advanced 2.7% and 1.7% respectively.

The decliners from the UK and Ireland were led by software firm Endava and biopharmaceutical company Akari Therapeutics , which dropped 3.1% and 1.9% respectively. They were followed by biopharmaceutical company Biodexa Pharmaceuticals and pharmaceutical company Silence Therapeutics , which lost 4.1% and 2.4% respectively.

data.title: Estrella Immunopharma Completes First Dose Group in Non-Hodgkin Lymphoma Study
data.description:
Estrella Immunopharma said Friday it has completed the first dose group in its ongoing phase I/II clinical trial evaluating EB103 in adult patients with relapsed/refractory B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma.

Based on preliminary data, no dose-limiting toxicities or serious adverse events related to treatment were observed in the group, with tumor response noted in all patients in the first month, the company said.

After reviewing safety and efficacy data, the Data and Safety Monitoring Board has approved the initiation of a higher dose group, the company added.

The company's shares were up 4% in recent trading.

data.title: Hims & Hers Health Shares Tumble After Novo Nordisk Says Wegovy, Ozempic Shortage is Over
data.description:
By Denny Jacob

Hims & Hers Health shares tumbled after a leading competitor said a shortage of its weight-loss treatments has been resolved, raising questions about the company's prospects in the space.

Shares tumbled 21% to $52.22. Over the last year, the stock has surged more than 450%.

Drugmaker Novo Nordisk said that the Food and Drug Administration determined that the shortage of Wegovy and Ozempic, the company's blockbuster obesity and diabetes drugs, has been resolved. It added that the regulator's assessment confirmed that the U.S. supply of these prescription-only GLP-1 medicines now meets or exceeds both current and projected U.S. demand.

The news appears to have alarmed Hims' investors, given the company's weight-loss offering filled a void left by a mismatch in supply and demand. The company last year began offering access to compounded semaglutide, a version of the active ingredient in Novo Nordisk's Wegovy and Ozempic drugs, at a far lower price than those branded drugs.

The FDA allows semaglutide to be compounded because it is considered to be in short supply. The regulator on Friday said it doesn't intend to take action against compounders to avoid unnecessary disruption to patient treatment.

Hims, a telehealth-consultation platform, has indicated it will stick with its weight-loss offerings even if GLP-1 drugs are taken off the FDA's shortage list. The company also focuses on treatments for sexual health, hair loss and mental health, among others.

Write to Denny Jacob at denny.jacob@wsj.com

data.title: Guardant Health Price Target Raised to $59.00/Share From $39.00 by Raymond James
Akamai Technologies Headed for Year of Transition While Near-term Remains Limited, BofA Says
data.description:
Akamai Technologies has "limited" short-term growth opportunities, however, the company's "muted" outlook suggests that this year will likely be one of transition, BofA Securities said in a note Friday.

Analysts, including Madeline Brooks, said they see restricted near-term catalysts as the company shifts from slower-growing areas like web security and legacy computing to faster-growing areas like Application Programming Interface security and Cloud Infrastructure Services. They added that while they remain optimistic about the long-term prospects, transitions take time.

The company's "muted" guidance for 2025, with a growth forecast of 3%, compared to the consensus estimate of 7%, suggests this will be a year of multiple transitions, the analysts said.

Akamai's new long-term targets do not predict a return to double-digit growth until 2029 at the earliest, compared to the consensus forecast of 2027, the analysts said, adding that while promising, new growth drives only account for 11% of revenues, which we forecast to grow to only 25% of revenues by 2027, "implying revenues will continue to feel the effects of decelerating growth in the near to mid-term," BofA analysts said.

BofA downgraded Akamai to neutral from buy and cut its price target to $100 from $125. Shares of the company fell past 15% in recent trading.

data.title: U.S. Activity Suffers Setback as Tariff Threats Hurt Sentiment — Update
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

U.S. economic activity suffered a setback this month, as uncertainty about government policies related to spending cuts and tariffs adversely affected business and consumer sentiment, according to surveys released Friday.

The S&P Global Flash U.S. Composite PMI--which gauges private-sector activity in the manufacturing and services sectors--sank to 50.4 in February from 52.7 in January, the weakest level in 17 months, fresh purchasing managers' index surveys said Friday. The reading above 50 suggests overall activity continued to expand.

"The upbeat mood seen among U.S. businesses at the start of the year has evaporated, replaced with a darkening picture of heightened uncertainty, stalling business activity and rising prices," S&P Global Market Intelligence Chief Business Economist Chris Williamson said.

A renewed fall in services output offset faster manufacturing growth, though that latter was in part linked to front-running of tariffs, hinting at only a temporary boost, S&P said.

The overall result dragged optimism from multiyear highs at the turn of the year to one of the gloomiest readings since the pandemic, Williamson continued.

Input cost pressures meanwhile spiked higher, a concern for the Federal Reserve that expressed little appetite for rate cuts in the near term, according to minutes of the January policy meeting published this week.

The U.S.'s downside surprise contrasts with Asian indicators that pointed to robust growth and European surveys that went sideways in February.

Whereas the PMI previously indicated sustained economic growth in excess of 2% late in 2024, it now signals fragile annualized growth of just 0.6%, Williamson noted.

Gloomier sentiment extended beyond firms. Consumer confidence slumped at the end of February, according to the University of Michigan's latest survey, as Americans swore off major purchases due to fears that tariff-induced price rises are imminent. Expectations for inflation for the year ahead spiked.

President Trump has pledged 25% tariffs on steel and aluminum imports, due to go into effect in March, and also this week said he would consider new tariffs of 25% or more on automobiles, semiconductors and pharmaceutical products. Trump so far has imposed 10% tariffs on all Chinese goods in addition to previous tariffs, and threatened 25% tariffs on Canada and Mexico before suspending them.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com
